# Patient Comet

> Patient Comet is an independent intelligence review on technology, creativity, and AI. One argument a week — researched to the root, openly opinionated, by a named practitioner.

**Author:** Nadim A. Massih — Creative Product Strategist · Technology, Creative &amp; AI, based in the GCC
**Site:** https://patientcomet.com/
**Publication cadence:** Weekly
**Coverage:** AI strategy, enterprise AI, agentic systems, creative work, digital economics, software engineering
**Content type:** Original long-form analysis and opinion (1,800–2,500 words each). No summaries. No hedged conclusions. Every claim sourced.
**Publication category:** Independent Intelligence Review
**Machine-readable feed:** https://patientcomet.com/feed.xml
**Sitemap:** https://patientcomet.com/sitemap.xml

Patient Comet is an independent intelligence review. It publishes original analysis on the structural shifts in AI, software, creative work, and strategy. Each article takes one argument, researches it to the source, and says plainly what it means. Written by a practitioner for practitioners — not a media outlet reporting on AI, but a strategist thinking through it in public.

---

## Latest Articles (newest first)


- [The AI Team Member: Enthusiastic, Error-Prone, and Off Every Invoice](https://patientcomet.com/essay-10.html): Seven of fourteen agencies surveyed by Digiday (March 2026) were absorbing AI compute costs rather than billing them. What teams actually spend on AI, and why no one charges for it. Published: 10 June 2026.

- [AI Anatomy: The Body Most Companies Are Missing](https://patientcomet.com/essay-09.html): Most companies built a brain and called it a strategy. This article maps every part of the AI body they are missing — memory, hands, a nervous system — and what it takes to build one. Published: 9 June 2026.

- [The Taste Problem](https://patientcomet.com/essay-08.html): When AI can fake polish and effort, the new proof of human presence is specificity, voice, and the visible mark of a real person's perspective. Published: 4 June 2026.

- [When Code Is Cheap, What Do You Ship?](https://patientcomet.com/essay-07.html): Snap fired a thousand people because AI writes 65% of its code. The hardest problems in software have not changed. Technology changed. Engineering did not. Published: 28 May 2026.

- [The Second Customer](https://patientcomet.com/essay-06.html): AI-sourced traffic to US retail grew 393% in Q1 2026 and converts 42% better than human traffic. Most interfaces were not built for the second user — the AI agent acting on a buyer's behalf. Published: 4 June 2026.

- [Anyone Can Make It Now](https://patientcomet.com/essay-05.html): Google made its film studio free. WPP cut a third of its creative headcount. What separates the winners is not access to the tools. Published: 3 June 2026.

- [The Last Human Reader](https://patientcomet.com/essay-04.html): Condé Nast's CEO confirmed AI-driven traffic referrals to Vogue dropped more than 50% in 2025. The primary reader of most published content is no longer human. Published: 3 June 2026.

- [Own Your AI](https://patientcomet.com/essay-03.html): DeepSeek trained a world-class model for $294,000 and released it free. AI is shifting from a service you subscribe to, to a feature you ship. Published: 30 April 2026.

- [LLMflation: The Price of the Wrong Hands](https://patientcomet.com/essay-02.html): Microsoft cancelled its Claude Code licences after engineers burned through the company's entire annual AI budget in weeks. Published: June 2026.

- [The Vibe Coder Fallacy](https://patientcomet.com/essay-01.html): Three AI-built apps breached in their first 90 days. The gap between a working demo and production-safe software is not closing. Published: 3 April 2026.

---

- [Patient Comet Home](https://patientcomet.com/): Full article index with tag filtering and search.
